evidence_items.snippet: The composition of the Ibuprofen-Loaded Formulations includes Ibuprofen at 1% (w/w), Ethyl oleate 10% (w/w), Cremophor® EL 5%, and Transcutol® P 5%. The in vitro transdermal permeation profiles demonstrated that the microemulsion formulation significantly improved the permeation of Ibuprofen compared to standard gel formulations.

The composition of the Ibuprofen-Loaded Formulations includes Ibuprofen at 1% (w/w), Ethyl oleate 10% (w/w), Cremophor® EL 5%, and Transcutol® P 5%. The in vitro transdermal permeation profiles demonstrated that the microemulsion formulation significantly improved the permeation of Ibuprofen compared to standard gel formulations.

provenance.route_notes: The study examines the development and efficacy of a new transdermal delivery formulation for ibuprofen using microemulsion systems.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Section: In vitro Transdermal Test

evidence_items.snippet: Ibuprofen (10, 100, 500 μM) and 8-Br-cAMP (1 mM) were dissolved in RA medium. Cells were cultured for 24 hours under standard conditions.

route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Fig 1. Ibuprofen increased neurite length of human model neurons. (A) Treatment with 100 μM and 500 μM Ibuprofen increased neurite outgrowth of human model neurons as measured after 24 hours.
Ibuprofen (10, 100, 500 μM) and 8-Br-cAMP (1 mM) were dissolved in RA medium. The ROCK inhibitor Y-27632 (1, 5, 10, 50 μM) was diluted to final concentration from a stock solution (10 mM) in H2O.
